rater_lens: More positive on agent leverage and model-improvement upside.
scope: Scoped to Figma's website design and publishing product rather than the full Figma suite.

context.rationale: Figma Sites reuses shared components, variables, and file context from the design workflow instead of starting from a blank website canvas.

trust.rationale: Sites inherits Figma's organization-level security and account controls, which matter for teams already standardizing on the broader platform.

distribution.rationale: Figma already sits inside many product and brand design teams, so Sites can piggyback on an existing collaboration footprint.

judgment.rationale: The tool preserves design intent well, but scarce website strategy and performance judgment still live largely with the team using it.

liability_governance.rationale: Team and org controls exist, but public evidence still points to lighter publishing governance than the more operations-heavy web platforms.

frequency.rationale: Teams that already work in Figma can use Sites in recurring launch and update cycles, though not every Figma user publishes sites regularly.

operational_dependence.rationale: Sites can matter materially for launch velocity, but it is not yet the default operating system for public web programs in the way deeper incumbents can be.

system_position.rationale: The product sits at the handoff point between design intent and publishable website output rather than only at ideation time.

switching_cost.rationale: Teams with established Figma systems gain reuse, but the published site layer remains portable enough that migration risk is still moderate.

budget_durability.rationale: Sites benefits from Figma's existing seat budget, which makes it more durable than a standalone experimental AI subscription.

model_improvement_test.rationale: Better models should improve iteration speed and content generation inside the same shared design context instead of bypassing it entirely.

wrapper_risk_test.rationale: Sites is deeper than a generator because it carries team context and design systems, but the web publishing surface is still young enough to face copycat pressure.

agent_readiness_test.rationale: The broader Figma stack already exposes strong design-to-code and agent-facing surfaces, which helps Sites fit agent-assisted workflows.

accountability_test.rationale: The product influences what gets shipped, but it does not own uptime, conversion, or downstream business execution once the site is live.

outcome_depth_test.rationale: Sites can accelerate launches and improve consistency, but real business outcomes still depend on external content, traffic, and experimentation loops.

limitations: This is a structured desk-research rating pass from one shared evidence packet, not an independent blinded human rater.
